brand.name: Port City 251
brand.vertical: Local media / community newsletter (Mobile, AL) + SMB advertising

summary: Port City 251 is a free weekly email newsletter covering hyperlocal Mobile, Alabama news — events, restaurant openings, civic decisions, and community stories — reaching 500+ subscribers and a 10,000+ member Facebook community. It serves two audiences: Mobile residents who want a curated local read, and local small businesses who buy affordable advertising packages (directory listings, business spotlights, banner ads, event listings) to reach that hyperlocal audience.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new subscriber to their first 'I almost didn't know about this' moment fast, and flag the advertising/directory opportunity to any business owners on the list.
campaigns.trigger: New newsletter signup (web form submission) — send instantly.
campaigns.segment: New subscribers; branch a single line for self-identified local business owners toward the free directory listing.
campaigns.rationale: New subscribers today get only a confirmation email and no guided first-week experience, yet welcome emails are the most-opened a brand sends — this is the cheapest, highest-engagement way to turn a passive signup into a habitual weekly opener for a 500-person list where every retained reader grows advertiser leverage.

emails.purpose: Warm neighborly welcome, set the weekly-read expectation, and deliver one quick local win immediately so the value lands before the first newsletter even arrives.
emails.subject: Welcome, neighbor — here's what's happening in Mobile this week
emails.preview: Your 5-minute Port City 251 read starts now. One local thing you'd have missed inside.
keyPoints: Open warm and local: 'You just joined 500+ neighbors keeping up with Mobile' — set the morning-coffee, 5-minute-read expectation
Deliver one quick win now: a single can't-miss local event, restaurant opening, or neighborhood story so the 'I almost didn't know about this' aha hits immediately
Promise no spam, one-click unsubscribe — directly answer the 'I already get too many emails' hesitation
Soft line for business owners: 'Run a local business? Get listed free in our directory' linking to the free listing
Real human reply-to and a friendly sign-off in first-person-plural community voice

campaigns.name: Advertiser Inquiry-to-Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a warm advertising inquiry that didn't close on the first touch into a paid package by handling the list-size/ROI objection and offering a low-commitment first step.
campaigns.trigger: Advertising contact-form submission with no close within ~3 days.
campaigns.segment: Business owners who inquired about advertising but haven't booked; branch by stated intent (spotlight vs. ongoing package).
campaigns.rationale: These are the hottest revenue leads the brand has — they already raised their hand — and right now inquiries that go cold after the form fill get no follow-up, leaving the most direct money on the table. A single objection-handling email closes the gap fastest.

emails.purpose: Answer the unspoken 'is 500 subscribers worth it?' objection head-on with the full-reach picture and social proof, then offer the lowest-friction way to start.
emails.subject: About reaching Mobile customers — a quick honest answer
emails.preview: It's not just 500 inboxes. Here's the real local reach, and an easy way to try it.
keyPoints: Lead by naming the objection plainly: '500 subscribers sounds small — here's why local businesses still get walk-ins from it'
Reframe true reach: 500+ engaged ~25%-open subscribers PLUS the 10,000+ member Facebook community — hyperlocal Mobile customers, not broad untargeted ad spend
Social proof: a short 'people walked in and said they saw us in Port City 251' style success line
Low-commitment offer: one-time Business Spotlight at $95 — no monthly contract, evaluate the response before committing to Bronze/Silver/Gold
Frictionless next step: reply to this email or grab a slot — a real person responds within 24 hours

campaigns.name: Free Directory Lister Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move free directory listers from a no-cost listing toward their first paid advertising package over a patient cycle.
campaigns.trigger: Business added to the free directory (no paid package purchased).
campaigns.segment: Free directory listers who are not yet advertisers — the highest-value warm-lead pool.
campaigns.rationale: Free listers have already self-identified as local businesses wanting community visibility but aren't being walked toward a paid conversation at all — converting even a handful into Bronze/Spotlight buyers is the most pressing growth gap the team named.

emails.purpose: Show proof that newsletter placement drives real local response, then make the low-risk Business Spotlight the obvious next step up from a free listing.
emails.subject: Your free listing is live — want Mobile to actually notice it?
emails.preview: A directory line gets found. A Business Spotlight gets remembered. Here's the difference.
keyPoints: Acknowledge the free listing and thank them for being part of the local business community
Contrast quietly: a directory line is great for people already searching; a Spotlight puts you in front of every engaged subscriber this week
Concrete proof point / mini-case of a local business that ran a Spotlight and saw calls or walk-ins
Frame audience honestly: hyperlocal Mobile readers + 10,000+ Facebook community — the neighbors most likely to become your customers
Single low-commitment CTA: $95 one-time Business Spotlight, no monthly contract — a natural first paid step before Bronze/Silver/Gold

campaigns.name: Subscriber Win-Back & Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate subscribers who have stopped opening, and sunset the truly dead to protect sender reputation.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days with no opens or clicks.
campaigns.segment: Dormant newsletter subscribers; lapsed advertisers handled as a separate branch.
campaigns.rationale: On a 500-person list every reactivated reader matters for advertiser leverage, and cleaning dead weight protects deliverability before the list scales — a quick, high-leverage flow to ship.

emails.purpose: Pattern-interrupt a dormant subscriber by reminding them of the core value and asking, neighbor-to-neighbor, whether they still want the weekly read.
emails.subject: Did we lose you, Mobile?
emails.preview: We've still got the events, openings, and local stories you signed up for. Want them?
keyPoints: Warm, no-guilt tone: 'We noticed you've been quiet — totally fine, life gets busy'
Remind of core value: the curated 5-minute Mobile read of events, restaurant openings, and neighborhood news they'd otherwise miss
Tease something fresh and recent to give a real reason to come back
One clear choice: a button to stay subscribed (re-confirm engagement)
Set up the sunset: 'If we don't hear back we'll stop cluttering your inbox' — honest, deliverability-protecting close

campaigns.name: Advertiser Renewal & Expansion
campaigns.objective: Protect monthly advertiser revenue and grow accounts from Bronze toward Silver/Gold and add-ons.
campaigns.trigger: Approaching the end of an advertiser's 4-issue/monthly package cycle; usage/engagement milestone signaling upsell readiness.
campaigns.segment: Active paying advertisers; branch high-response accounts toward expansion and low-response accounts toward a save/help conversation.
campaigns.rationale: Advertiser packages are the brand's only recurring revenue and there's no current renewal or upgrade prompt, so churn from unproven ROI goes unaddressed — a recap-plus-expansion email both saves and grows the account.

emails.purpose: Quantify the local response their ad generated this cycle, then make renewing-and-upgrading the natural next move.
emails.subject: Here's what your ad did in Mobile this month
emails.preview: Your reach this cycle — plus an easy way to do even more next month.
keyPoints: Open with a concrete recap of their placement's reach: impressions across the newsletter and 10,000+ Facebook community this cycle — directly answers the ROI/attribution worry
Tie it to outcomes: remind them this is the audience that walks in saying 'I saw you in Port City 251'
Make the renewal effortless: month-to-month, no long contract, continue without a gap
Expansion path for engaged advertisers: Bronze to Silver/Gold for more placement and monthly analytics, or add a Business Spotlight or Facebook Post
Save branch for low-response accounts: offer a quick call to improve the creative or placement before they consider stopping
